2605.28860.title: Mechanistic origins of catastrophic forgetting: why RL preserves circuits better than SFT?
2605.28860.paper_summary: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) frequently induces catastrophic forgetting of prior capabilities. Recent work has shown that reinforcement learning (RL) retains prior capabilities more effectively than supervised fine-tuning (SFT), attributing this to policy-gradient updates remaining closer to the base policy \cite{shenfeld2025rl}. We extend this behavioral account to the mechanistic level and ask whether RL's advantage is mirrored by stronger preservation of internal computational circuits. We introduce differential circuit vulnerability, a head-level measure of how much a circuit degrades under fine-tuning, and use it to compare RL and SFT on Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct adapted to scientific question-answering. We find a clear mechanistic trade-off: SFT adapts more rapidly to the target task but produces substantially greater circuit disruption and forgetting of prior capabilities, whereas RL preserves a larger fraction of the base circuit at the cost of slower task adaptation. These findings suggest that circuit preservation may help explain why RL is more robust to catastrophic forgetting. We released our code here: https://github.com/rl-sft-circuit-research/differential-circuit-vulnerability.
2605.28860.paper_summary_zh: 微調大型語言模型（LLMs）經常會導致之前能力的災難性遺忘。最近的研究顯示，強化學習（RL）比監督微調（SFT）更有效地保留之前的能力，這歸因於策略梯度更新更接近基礎策略 \cite{shenfeld2025rl}。我們將這一行為解釋擴展到機制層面，並詢問RL的優勢是否反映在內部計算電路的更強保護上。我們引入了差異電路脆弱性，這是一種衡量電路在微調下退化程度的頭部級別指標，並用它來比較在科學問答適應的Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct上進行的RL和SFT。我們發現了一個明顯的機制權衡：SFT對目標任務的適應速度更快，但會產生顯著更大的電路破壞和之前能力的遺忘，而RL則在較慢的任務適應代價下保留了更大比例的基礎電路。這些發現表明，電路保護可能有助於解釋為什麼RL對災難性遺忘更具魯棒性。我們在這裡發布了我們的代碼：https://github.com/rl-sft-circuit-research/differential-circuit-vulnerability。
2605.28860.author: Jeanmely Rojas Nunez et.al.
2605.28860.authors: Jeanmely Rojas Nunez, Viraj Sawant, Nathan Allen, Nomgondalai Amgalanbaatar, Yannis Zongo, Vasu Sharma, Maheep Chaudhary
